methods.description: Process for collecting sea ice samples during Polish Antarctic Expeditions and BIOMASS III study for analysis of interior community and diatom discoloration.

steps.description: Sea ice samples used in this study were collected during the 7th and 11th Polish Antarctic Expeditions to King George Island in winter 1983 (8 samples) and summer 1986 (one sample), respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sea ice samples during Polish Antarctic Expeditions

steps.description: Samples were obtained of the interior community in drifting sea ice that had become grounded on the shore of Admiralty Bay, near the Polish Antarctic Station 'H. Arctowski' at approximately 62°10' S, 58°28' W.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain samples from grounded drifting sea ice near Admiralty Bay

steps.description: Two samples were also collected during the BIOMASS III study on board the RV 'Profesor Siedlecki' from the northern limit of the sea ice at the Weddell-Scotia Confluence.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples during BIOMASS III study from Weddell-Scotia Confluence

steps.description: After collection, the surface of the sea ice was washed in filtered sea water to remove plankton cells.
steps.searchable_summary: wash sea ice surface with filtered sea water to remove plankton cells
